_definition.ISO_639_3_eng: The set of international telephone calling codes assigned to a country or territory by the ITU.

_description.ISO_639_3_eng: Values are numeric strings prefixed with `+` (e.g. `+39` for Italy, `+1` for the United States). A country may have more than one calling code; equally, a single calling code may be shared among multiple countries or territories. The set contains all codes that can reach the country when dialling internationally.
